text.title: Boundary Enforcement Tools
text.summary: Module boundaries decay without automated enforcement. Wire a boundary linter into CI as a required gate. Tool choice depends on language: import-linter for Python, ArchUnit/Spring Modulith for Java, depguard for Go, dependency-cruiser for TypeScript.

rule.statement: Configure import-linter with an independence contract that forbids direct cross-module imports, and a layers contract that enforces clean architecture within modules. Run lint-imports in CI as a required gate.
rule.rationale: Python has no language-level module visibility. Without tooling, any developer can import any class from any module and the violation only appears at code review — if caught at all.

rule.statement: For plain Java: write ArchUnit tests verifying that internal packages are not accessed from outside their module. For Spring Boot: use Spring Modulith's ApplicationModules.of(App.class).verify() test — it runs at test time and fails the build on boundary violations.
rule.rationale: Spring Modulith provides first-class modular monolith support: boundary verification, documentation generation, and event publication tracking in one framework.

rule.statement: Place each module under an internal/ subdirectory to leverage Go's compiler-enforced visibility. Add depguard rules to golangci-lint to block cross-module internal imports at lint time.
rule.rationale: Go's internal/ package is the only language-enforced boundary mechanism. Without it, any package in the same module can import any other. depguard adds explicit rules for cases the compiler does not catch.

example.code: # .golangci.yml linters-settings: depguard: rules: main: deny: - pkg: "myapp/orders/internal" desc: "Orders internals are private; use orders.Service interface" - pkg: "myapp/payments/internal" desc: "Payments internals are private; use payments.Service interface"

rule.statement: Use dependency-cruiser with forbidden rules blocking cross-module internal imports. Run depcruise in CI to catch violations before merge.
rule.rationale: TypeScript has no package-level visibility. dependency-cruiser provides path-based rules that approximate module boundaries without requiring separate npm packages per module.

list.item: Module imports internal class from another module → immediate boundary violation, fix before merge
Cross-schema SQL JOIN detected → refactor to use module API or denormalize at write time
Shared mutable state between modules → extract to owner module
Circular dependency between modules → break with events or extract shared concept
Module > 50 files → consider splitting into two bounded contexts
Module < 5 files → consider merging into a neighboring module
